named_technique: Native_decide cascade on Finset.range 5001 with small-r/large-r dichotomy (slot744 v2 + R3 iter5 DNA)
seminal_paper_arxiv: internal:submissions/nu4_final/r3_ft_iter5_extracted/517a3f5d-a15b-4e1d-8507-6fa8035749ea_aristotle/RequestProject/Main.lean (R3 iter5, q ≤ 2000, UUID 517a3f5d, May 30 2026) + internal:submissions/nu4_final/ft_q1583_v2_extracted/.../Main.lean (slot744 v2, large-r branch at q=1583, UUID 940d518b). Underlying mechanism: Wieferich-type Fermat reduction (multiplicative order of 3 modulo a prime divisor of (q^3-1)/(q-1)) — cf. Le 2012 for the p=3 elementary cases and the classical Feit-Thompson 1962 conjecture statement.
bridge_lemma: Extend R3's 14-prime native_decide table to 28 primes q ≡ 71 (mod 72) in (3, 5000], with large-r branch (A(q) prime) for q ∈ {71, 1583, 2663, 3671, 4751} where A(q) ∈ {5113, 2.5M, 7.1M, 13.5M, 22.6M} all under 2^25 (fits u64) and small-r branch for the remaining 23 primes. R3 iter5's `suffices h : ∀ q ∈ Finset.range N, ...; native_decide` template lifts N=2001 → 5001 unchanged. Heartbeat scaling: iter5 (14 primes, max 22-bit) compiled in ~11s under 8M, so iter6 doubles primes + max 25-bit modulus and requires maxHeartbeats bump to 40-80M.
informal_proof_outline: Step 1: Enumerate family primes q ≡ 71 (mod 72), prime, 3 < q ≤ 5000 — yields 28 primes (computed above). Step 2: Reduce universal statement to Finset.range 5001 via Nat.ModEq → q%72=71 conversion (R3 iter5 template, lines 10-19). Step 3: native_decide evaluates each candidate q: rejects non-prime / q≤3 / q%72≠71 in O(1), then for each family prime performs one modular exponentiation 3^q mod r where r is the smallest prime factor of A(q) (small-r branch, 23 primes) or r := A(q) itself (large-r branch, 5 primes). All 28 results ≠ 1, witnessing ¬ A(q) ∣ (3^q-1)/2 in each case. Step 4: Dichotomy is invisible to the tactic — both branches reduce to identical native_decide arithmetic; the conceptual split explains *why* the bound q ≤ 5000 is feasible (max modulus is 22.6M, fits in u64).
candidate_lemmas: native_decide_budget_5000: max A(q) at q=5000 is 25005001 (< 2^25), so each modexp 3^q mod A(q) is at most ~28 squarings on a 25-bit modulus; 28 primes × ~28 ops/prime ≈ 800 modexp steps, well within maxHeartbeats 80000000.

large_r_branch_5000: A(q) is prime for q ∈ {71, 1583, 2663, 3671, 4751}; A-values {5113, 2507473, 7094233, 13479913, 22576753}. The slot744 v2 mechanism (r := A(q), Fermat reduction direct) handles each via the unified native_decide template.
small_r_branch_5000: for the remaining 23 primes, A(q) is composite with smallest prime factor r « A(q); native_decide finds r and computes 3^q mod r via Fermat-reduction with exponent q mod (r-1).
template_lift_invariance: R3 iter5's `Finset.range 2001` → `Finset.range 5001` is a purely numerical edit; no proof restructuring required.
honest_disclaimer: iter6 of slot720 family (slot720 → slot736 → slot744 v2 → R3 iter5 → iter6). Direct extension of R3 iter5 (UUID 517a3f5d, May 30 2026) from q ≤ 2000 (14 primes) to q ≤ 5000 (28 primes). Adds 14 new family primes including 3 new large-r primes {2663, 3671, 4751}. Novelty is scaling validation: confirms native_decide template scales to ~25-bit moduli and ~28 primes. CANNOT: prove FT for p=3 in full generality — this is a finite-bound extension. The dichotomy on Nat.Prime A(q) is real (5 of 28 primes are large-r), but invisible to native_decide. fit_score = 0.6 reflects: (i) R3 iter5 template is proven to scale (it discharged 14 primes); (ii) max modulus grows 9x (2.5M → 22.6M), still well within u64; (iii) heartbeat budget likely needs 5-10x bump but no structural change. paired_llm='claude-only' (template extension, no debate needed).
